rules.title: The lane anchors to a real issue
rules.lesson: ADR-0009 'no issue, no work' — the anchor is what lets leases, takeovers, and divergence derive; an anchor-less branch is invisible to all three
rules.rationale: The ref IS the claim (FS2): a lane whose name parses to no issue can't be joined to intent, and under strict anchoring an anchor that doesn't resolve at the forge is a claim on nothing. Existence + resolution only — whether the issue is still OPEN is DIV-01's finding, not this rule's (no overlap). The descriptor `anchoring` knob governs: off SKIPs, relaxed wants a parseable anchor, strict also wants forge resolution. Lands warn; M7 promotes per posture.
rules.fix: Claim lanes through the tool — baseline lane claim <issue>. If this repo genuinely doesn't anchor, set descriptor anchoring to 'off' (relaxed still requires a parseable anchor).

rules.title: A lane branch carries its own session record
rules.lesson: C14/C12 — records ride the branch to main (ADR-0009 Rule 3): the forensic tier lands atomically with the change it describes
rules.rationale: A lane that merges without its session record leaves the next orient blind to why the change happened and what was left open. The record travels in the same PR — the 'update the status' PR class must not exist.
rules.fix: Write the record on this branch: baseline log -m "what happened" --next "the one next step". Lands warn; M7 promotes per posture.

rules.title: The lane's session record has a filled-in next:
rules.lesson: C06/ADR-0009 — next: is the pause-state a stateless successor resumes from; an empty next: is a record that stops one sentence short of being useful
rules.rationale: Fires ONLY on a present record whose next: is empty — a missing record entirely is FLOW-02's finding (no overlap, no double report). The generalized session-log guard from the ADR-0009 prototype's check-session-log.mjs. Lands warn; M7 promotes per posture.
rules.fix: Re-log with the one next step: baseline log -m "..." --next "the one next step" (the newest record governs).

rules.title: The branch sits in a declared family
rules.lesson: CF-scope (M5 panel, capped first-to-drop) — a namespace only means something if the repo's REAL branch inventory is declared; otherwise every legitimate release/* branch is wallpaper
rules.rationale: Placement against lanes.namespace + lanes.families (additive): the demo's real inventory (adopt/*, release/*) declares itself instead of warning forever. A branch outside every declared family is either an undeclared family (declare it) or a stray (claim a lane). Lands warn; M7 promotes per posture.
rules.fix: Claim a lane (baseline lane claim <issue>) or declare the family in the descriptor: "lanes": { "families": ["release/*"] }.

rules.title: The newest session record is pushed
rules.lesson: ADR-0009 Rule 5 — origin is the rendezvous; a record only the local disk knows is invisible to every other lane and to reclaim's lease derivation
rules.rationale: The arbitrated threshold-free predicate (M5 ruling): the NEWEST session record exists locally but is absent at origin — session-boundary-aligned, zero invented tuning surface (no age windows, no counts). Judged against the last-fetched origin state and says so. Lands warn; M7 promotes per posture.
rules.fix: Push the lane: git push (the record rides the branch — ADR-0009 Rule 3).

rules.title: A gated subject changes with its record in the same PR
rules.lesson: CF9 — C14 same-PR atomicity was enforced for sessions only; the descriptor is V2's largest stored intent (DESC-03's blocker enforcement lands at M6 admit)
rules.rationale: A descriptor change is a posture change — the judgment saying why belongs in the same range, not a follow-up promise. Heuristic (subject→record mapping is a convention), so warn is its ceiling (C07). Check-context only since M6a: DESC-03 owns the same predicate at admit (disjoint contexts — one predicate judged once per run).
rules.fix: Add the corresponding record to this branch — for baseline.repo.json changes: baseline jdg new --kind deviation --subject "baseline.repo.json" --reason "..." --review-by <date>.

pairs.note: baseline.repo.json changed with no judgment record in the same range (DESC-03 preview)

rules.title: The lane's lease is live
rules.lesson: C31 — leases are fully derived (max of tip committedDate and PR updatedAt vs lease_ttl); a dead agent must never wedge a namespace
rules.rationale: Warns ONLY at derived ABANDONED (the ruling: STALE is orient's nudge, not a finding — wallpaper is forbidden). The same derive/lanes answer orient renders and reclaim gates on: one derivation, three surfaces. Lands warn; M7 promotes per posture.
rules.fix: Renew the lease by pushing work, or hand the lane over: baseline lane reclaim <issue> (from the taking-over agent).
